close_reason: Requires Doppler account and service token for integration testing. Code is correct by inspection.
description:
src/lib.rs:253-260 — the crate unconditionally submits a WritableBackendRegistration so umbrella-crate URI dispatch will route 'secretx:doppler:...' put operations through this code. The module doc (src/lib.rs:5-8) acknowledges 'Not yet integration-tested.' Combined with finding #1 (wrong endpoint), this means any user who tries to use put will fail in production with a confusing 4xx error.

Maintainance consequence: every issue report will say 'doppler put returns HTTP 404' and you'll have to triage them as the same bug. Worse, if/when finding #1 is fixed but tests are still skipped, a future regression in the request body shape (e.g. an API change requiring change_requests for some accounts) will not be caught.

Fix scope: this finding is about the gap, not the bug itself. Either (a) gate WritableBackendRegistration behind a feature flag until put is verified, (b) explicitly document the non-tested status in the put rustdoc with #[doc(hidden)] or a #[deprecated] note, or (c) commit to running the integration test in CI with a Doppler account before next release.
title: WritableBackendRegistration registered for Doppler with put never integration-tested
